review_summary: I like how the crop top s
review_text: I like how the crop top sweaters sit right at the waist of the fit and flare dresses. I'm VERY pleased with my firstever ModCloth purchase. I got this dress on sale for Black Friday, for a great price! It shipped out about 2 days after ordering, and arrived less than a week later. The dress is very soft, with a nice cottonblend feeling to it, but it is a bit thin. Depending on what bra I'm wearing under it, you can sometimes see the outline of the bra under the material of the dress. But the dress moves well, and fits me perfectly. It's a *tad* shorter (especially in the back  I have a booty!) than I would like it to be, but since a few other reviews mentioned this, I was warned. The color isn't as perfect a true red as it looks online  depending on the light, it sometimes has a slight pick tint to it, but it's pretty mild. The pockets lay very nicely, which is great, since I'm really curvy. Overall, I'm thrilled!

review_summary: I REALLY wanted to like t
review_text: I REALLY wanted to like this dress  I even spent extra on shipping because I ordered the M right when it came in stock and then the L a few minutes later (I was afraid it'd go out of stock again for a long time!). I'm usually a M in dresses, but the reviews seemed to indicate it ran small, so I wanted to get the L just in case. The M fit, but if I had been at ALL bustier than I am (I'm a 34 B), it would've been too tight. Unfortunately, it just wasn't as flattering as I'd hoped. I think the problem might have been that the waist was several inches higher than my natural waist and the dress was a couple inches shorter than I'd expected (came about three inches above my knee). So instead of getting a slim waistline and lengthened legs, the immediate effect was a short torso and awkwardly largelooking butt. I loved the color (a vibrant mustard), but I think it would be more flattering on someone who is shorter, or at least someone with a shorter torso.

This is my first Modcloth order. I bought a lot of different styles and brands to see what might 'stick.' The Modcloth brand seems to be a great fit. I am 5'10 and about 170. I don't have a super long inseam but often with dresses, they are too short overall arena too short in the torso, especially with 'unique' brands. This fit perfect in a L. One button gaps a little but can be fixed with fashion tape. I can see how the pattern might not work for everyone. I am am bit on the fence but think it would look cute with offwhite or brown oxfords. Anyway, I am sending it back because when I opened the bag, it smelled. I thought it was the fabric, but in getting it unfolded and such, I caught another whiff and it was strongest in the armpit area  definite BO. Super gross and icky. I am sure it would come out if I kept and washed it, but, ewww..... really??

review_summary: The cut and fit of these
review_text: The cut and fit of these jeans is amazing. Like most other reviewers have said they're flattering, high waisted without being mom jeans, comfortable. I originally bought these in a size 11. They 11 fit perfectly, but continued to shrink and shrink with each wash/dry. Eventually they became uncomfortably small. But I loved the fit so much I ordered again in a size 13. They were initially slightly roomy, shrunk up nicely after the first wash, and then have again continued to shrink with each following wash. They still fit, but I hope they stop shrinking soon. After years of abstaining from jeans, I wear these several times per week and would like to continue to do so! I'm admittedly hard on my jeans, I wash them often and always put them in the dryer. I just don't have the patience or time to hang dry them. If you're nicer to your jeans you probably won't have the shrinkage issue.

This coat is beautiful! I love the fabric, the color, THE HOOD! I typically wear a size 16 or an XL, depending on a number of factors. I decided to size up to a 1X, since a number of the XLs I've ordered from Modcloth have been just the tiniest bit too small (wearable, but snug), and some of the reviews suggested sizing up. I swim in the 1X size, but it's too late to exchange it, as I need it for a trip to Oregon next week (I live in Arizona, and haven't needed a coat for a long time). The waist tie is high, but I have a very high waist, so it doesn't bother me. Ladies with longer torsos ma find this odd though.Overall, I feel like this coat is going to keep me warm and happy and feeling pretty even with the sizing issue.

review_summary: This is a great shade of
review_text: This is a great shade of red! For being flimsy material the blouse is well constructed and washes well! I was really worried when I read previous reviews for this skirt, but it turned out alright in the end.I've been looking for a midlength Kelly green skirt forever, and I settled on this one after a few disappointments with others. The color is exactly as shown, and although the fabric is wrinkly, a good ironing can fix that.I had ordered a Small, and when I pulled it out of the bag, I was worried that maybe I should have sized up and gotten a Medium, but it only LOOKED like it wouldn't fit. Thanks to the elastic back part of the waistband, the skirt was easy to put on, and fits a little snugly, not uncomfortably tight at all.I've seen people complain about the belt being of poor quality, but it looks fine to me; it looks no more different than similar belts I've seen in stores.The only thing I'll have to do to this skirt is to hem it to hit maybe a little below my knees or right at. Untouched, the hem hits a little lower than midcalf. I'm petite sized when it comes to height, and I suppose that this skirt's length works for people who are a bit taller. But then I could leave it alone, I haven't decided. But for sure, I'm keeping this skirt!

Some things I'd suggest if your considering this dress: size up and have it altered, if your over a C cup have the bra padding removed, have the sleeves taken in. This dress is all lace so there is no give. There are several layers of tulle in the skirt..so if your not using this as a wedding dress I'd suggest having some layers removed. Also as others have stated it does get snagged a bit...but I can overlook that.

review_summary: I was looking at all the

review_summary: So can I just say that th
review_text: So can I just say that this is the best money that I have ever invested into jeans?  EVER.  I'm not kidding.  I am always worried about buying jeans online because I am a tall gal.  I'm a little under 6', and let me tell you, it is not easy finding jeans that fit well when you are that tall of a female.  So of course, I was nervous about these jeans.  However, they fit PERFECTLY from the moment that I tried them on.  Even days later (I have worn them every day since they've arrived) they fit and feel incredible.  Not only are these great fitting jeans, they are amazingly comfortable.  I seriously think that I will buy more of these jeans, and they will be the only jeans that I ever wear.  I love them more than all of the jeans that I got to try on first, in the store.  You should definitely buy a size up from what you usually get.  The pant size that I normally purchase on ModCloth is size 11, and so I bought a size 13 in these pants.  They are form fitting, but not skin tight (which is how I like it).  Seriously, if you ever buy any jeans again, buy these ones.

This is the most beautiful dress I own, and I own a lot of beautiful dresses. I wore it for the first time last week and got compliments everywhere I went. I recently dyed my hair and the red hue looks stunning with the plum color of this dress. The only drawback to this dress is that it's a little long  I'm 5'2 and it hit below my knee. I'm wearing it to work so it's fine, but I just wish it was a little shorter so I could wear flats with it and still look young and flirty. I have to wear super high heels with it( to elimiinate the Amish feel), which is brutal on my feet. But otherwise, I love the look, feel, and style of this dress. Highly recommend it.

I don't hate it enough to send it back though. I think with the right type of bottoms it'll look okay.

I bought this coat 2 years ago when I moved from Houston (not much of a winter) to Denver (definitely a place that has winter). It has been 2 years and I still love it. The sleeves are a perfect length. The color is lovely and has brought me many compliments. The coat itself still looks great and keeps me warm. This was definitely a great choice for me. And the pockets! So many women's things have sad little useless pockets. The amount of stuff I can fit in the pockets of this coat is astounding. And because of the pleated skirt nature of the lower half of the coat, it doesn't make it look like my pockets are bulging and overstuffed (unless I REALLY overstuff them).

review_summary: Maybe I'm a fool for orde
review_text: Maybe I'm a fool for ordering this jacket twice. The first one I bought, I wore for a year. I wore it as my main coat and I live in Chicago so that means every day for several months. The insides became torn. The lining in the back came off. The pockets ripped. I figured I was a little hard on it. Trying to fit layers under a coat can be a challenge. So, since I liked the way it looked so much, this year I bought a second one but this time a size up. I've had it since October but I've only been wearing it regularly since December. The zipper is ripping at the bottom and the pockets have holes in them already. This coat is probably fine if it's a once in a while wear, but it is not high quality enough to be an every day coat. It's expensive, which makes the quality issue disappointing.

review_summary: This is the best hoodie I
review_text: This is the best hoodie I have ever bought!  Perfect for rainy, cool days when you don't want to wear a full coat.  It does bunch around the waist sometimes, but nothing a quick pull down can't fix.  The snaps on the wrist sometimes fling off if I catch it on something, but that is also a quick fix.  I do wish there were full pockets that keep stuff secure, but I can put my iphone in them and it stays, (although I probably wouldn't ride around on a bike or run with it in there).  It looks super bad ass, and looks awesome with cute flats, skinny jeans and the headband ModCloth sent with it!

Literally my favorite ModCloth purchase, and I've purchased a lot! This coat is so perfect, its the perfect weight for a California winter, the fabric is soft and wonderful, the hood is a little big but aren't they always? I love that its removable as well. review_summary: I wanted to love this swe
review_text: I wanted to love this sweater. It looks great in photos and looks great on other people. After waiting for it to come back in stock for weeks, I eagerly ordered this sweater and sat by the door for it to come in. Pros: Soft, comfortable, warm. Cons: Doesn't fit properly and pills easily. It's a drapey, slouchy sweater. But it wants to be a structured jacket. It looks weird zipped up (halfway, partway, or all the way), but why pay $55 for a sweater that doesn't utilize its main functions? The magnet catches on necklaces. It pilled after one day of use. It might look amazing on a girl with different proportions, but it certainly didn't do any favors for my pear shape. When I buy clothes from Modcloth, I usually get a ton of compliments. This was a meh sweater. I really wanted to love this sweater, but it just didn't work out, so I'm sadly returning it.

review_summary: I have this dress in gree
review_text: I have this dress in green and black and love it. I read a lot of reviews regarding size and I was unsure if I should go with the 8uk or 10uk. I went with the 10uk because there is nothing more uncomfortable than a dress that is too small. I think I could have pulled off the 8uk but I am still happy with the 10. I am usually a small in most dresses and tops or a medium if it's tight fitting. The material is flattering on the body and starts to flare out at the perfect spot. I got plenty of compliments from the green dress in one day!

My biggest problem with these jeans is the dye. Otherwise, they'd be a staple for sure. But the dye bleeds like crazy, and stains whichever purse I'm carrying. I tried soaking them in cold water and vinegar and then rinsing with cold water to lock in the dye, but no luck. A lighter color of these would probably be a better option. Also, I find the high waist to be a bit uncomfortable when sitting, but that's obviously just a personal preference. Otherwise very comfy.

review_summary: I really wanted to love t
review_text: I really wanted to love this skirt.  I read through the reviews and looked at all the user submitted pictures and decided to give it a chance.I was looking for a few more pencil skirts to wear to work.  This skirt was really form fitting. I'm a m/l but ordered this is large and the grey in XL (in hopes one would work and I could return one for the appropriate size) and neither were at all work appropriate. The material leaves no room for imperfections.  The fabric clings to your body  and is thin so it clings to every curve.  Which for a night out  or a date that might be fine but not at all appropriate for work attire. (I'll admit my butt did look good.)  I ended up returning the skirt. Honestly, the skirt would have probably worked out better had the fabric been thicker or made of another material.Obviously, this skirt works for some people, it was just my luck it didn't work for me.

review_summary: LOVE this coat. I live in
review_text: LOVE this coat. I live in Arizona, so I don't have much need for something this heavy, but on a recent winter trip to Europe needed something to keep this desert dweller warm! The insert of the collar is a great detail and is very cute, but if you were to functionally use it to keep your neck warm it gets sort of weirdyou'd have to zip the coat up to hold it in place (it kind of lays flat generally) and I think it would look awkward. For the days I wanted to stay extra bundled I just popped the knit insert off (buttons are tiny bit of a hassle so I wouldn't want to do this daily but it wasn't a deal breaker) and added a scarf. Still got the cute detail of the big collar but stayed warm. Boom!The fit is great. If you're curvy, you should know it's a pretty straight fit from bust to hips, but for me it worked really well (even though I'm plus size I have zero hips and the cut was perfect for me). It worked with everything from a tshirt to a light sweater underneath.  I would buy this coat again in a heartbeat!

review_summary: I really wanted to love t
review_text: I really wanted to love this, because I have been searching for a cropped cardigan to wear with my fit and flare dresses for a long time.  First of all, I normally wear a medium in most clothes, and sometimes a small, but this sweater in large fit pretty snug.  It actually looked good, though, except for the sleeves.  With the fact that it was cropped and a bit snug, the 3/4 sleeves made it look like I was wearing a child's sweater.  I am tall and have long arms, so I do avoid 3/4 sleeves normally, but I thought I would give it a go because I need a cropped cardigan.  Modcloth: make these with long sleeves and you will have customers, I guarantee it!  Another note, both sweaters had a rancid smell to them.  The navy was worse than the red.  It was so bad, I thought maybe one of them had been worn and returned stinky, but I think it was the shipping bags.

review_summary: Great Fit for Plus Sized Ladies!
review_text: Periodically I like to try out a pair of jeans in a brand different from my standard goto.  So when I saw that Modcloth was carrying a new line of jeans, I thought I'd give it a go.   Originally, I mistakenly ordered the 33 length jeans, as I typically fit an average length in other brands, but they were far too long!  However, I exchanged the jeans for 30 pair and they fit PERFECTLY, particularly in the waist, hips and thighs  not too tight and not too baggy.   Also, even though these jeans are a medium wash (I typically prefer a dark wash) the indigo coloring is still nice and rich in color and seemed to hold up at least through the first wash.  The only negative I can provide about this pair is that post wash, the bottom hem has curled up and creased as per standard with a pair of medium to light weight denim jeans, and will require ironing.

It looked okay with a cropped jacket (pictured), but ultimately I decided not to purchase the dress since I could really only style it that way.  My recommendation: Maybe not the best option for busty gals unless you want to go fully strapless for a bra.

I ordered this dress and the aisle be there as possible wedding dresses, and ended up going with the aisle be there. The bodice of this dress just wasn't as flattering as I'd have liked. I have fairly broad shoulders, and the tiny straps emphasized that somewhat. The padded bra cups are also slightly visible through the fabric, and made my chest look larger in a notsoflattering way. Zipping the dress up also requires either extreme flexibility or a buddy. I'm glad I got the dress to try on, but it's not the right wedding dress for me. However, I would consider getting this in the navy blue for a different event. I think the chest would look much better in a darker color.

The bottom half of the dress felt slightly too snug and the top half looked tentlike.  It seems a lot better worn with a belt but I just felt too selfconscious in it.  Also if you order the bat dress, it's worth noting that the color shown online is completely different than how the dress is in person.  Yes, I know that all monitors are calibrated differently (I color correct photography at my job and am very aware!) but the dress I saw online had a clear purple tint to it whereas the dress I received was inching more towards burgundy.  As a goth person who already doesn't have that many options with ModCloth... this simply didn't go at all with my aesthetic and had to go back.

After reading dozens of reviews on this dress, I had a really hard time deciding what size to order since my measurements fall accross a few sizes.  I settled on a small, which fits perfectly.  I agree with a lot of people who were a little let down by the button holes.  They will need to be reinforced unless you've got a small bust and (for me) the collar lays better with the top button undone.  The sleeves are poofy as was mentioned several times, but it's not bad enough to bother me (it's a little drafty).  This dress is really flattering for anyone with an hourglass shape, but be warned, the waist is small.  It zips up (left side) like a glove, but I would suggest sizing up if you like to have some extra breathing room in the rib/waist area.  Overall, I love this dress, and I love wearing it.  The length is perfect, but a little sheer so I don't leave the house without a slip and my boyfriend particularly likes the heartshaped buttons.  I've purchased several dresses from ModCloth and this one is definitely my favorite :)

review_summary: The fit is great on me.

The torso fits quite closely, but doesn't gape at the buttonholes at all.I'm fairly tall, and it hit just below the knee.  Shorter girls might need to hem it a bit.As several people have mentioned, the sleeves are a little funkythe arm hole is pretty low, and the cap sleeve gapes somewhat. I will likely try to alter the sleeves a bit, though, so they don't do the gape thing.Full disclosure, I also switched out the buttons for regular round ones. This was my first purchase for myself from Modcloth and I was happily surprised! I don't usually fill out reviews, but this is an exception. As a person, who finds it so difficult to find a pair of jeans and dreads shopping for pants in general, I found these to be a perfect fit. My shape is somewhat pearish so high waisted really works for me. I usually wear a size 67 in pants and am not a big fan of uber tight jeans or low rise so I sized up to a 9 and the size fits exactly how I like. The length was a little long but not too much.  Also, the pants are a wellmade garment and made in the USA something I definitely appreciate. I have yet to wear these jeans out for its first full day, but I think things will bode well. This is my first pair of high waisted jeans and I will probably invest in more...after I save some money ;)

review_summary: I tried this on at the Mo
review_text: I tried this on at the Modcloth shop. It's super cute in person. I had real issues with the sizing though. The difference between an L and an XL is comical. The XL was huge and extremely unflattering. It's not fitted and it doesn't come in at the waist at all. It looked like a giant sack. As I tend to be an XL in jackets I was surprised. So I tried the L. It was cut beautifully, flattering tailoring but *much* smaller. While the XL swam on me, the L was so tight across my bust I couldn't zip it all the way up. I have a larger chest, so it's not a new problem, but the difference in size and cut blew me away. I found the color and feel of the material slightly different on the XL too. I would recommend this if you're looking for a size L or below. But the cut and quality are not replicated in the larger sizes. A real shame as it is very cute.

review_summary: I was so hesitant to pay
review_text: I was so hesitant to pay full price for this dress. I won't say that I'm totally disappointed, but the quality was lacking in my opinion. There were several threads that were missewn and hanging about, but were easy to cut away without harming the bird detail. The skirt has plastic running through the hem to keep that nice shape, but on mine, where the pieces were sewn together, there were about two inches of extra plastic hanging off the hem on each layer of the skirt (there are 3 i think). Overall the construction was a bit lazy in the finishing and the material is a little cheap feeling. BUT it fit very well and the design is rather unique. There is NO zipper, which freaked me out at first because it looked so tiny out of the box, but the elastic on the back stretches to fit just fine. The dress also comes with adjustable spaghetti straps that you can attach if you want some extra security. I wore it for NYE and it was a hit. I knew all the little things that were wrong, but nobody else did so i guess it's all good. Overall, I love the look and the dress, but I wish the price point were better considering the quality.

I wanted to have a colorful pair of leggings because my collection is black and grey. The pros to the leggings are the design, color, and fit. The design is really good with the stars. The blues are bright and really pop with the Simply on a Saturday tunic that I paired with these leggings. The top is very long and covers up any chance of the lighter colors being a bit sheer. The fit is also very good for me. The leggings are supposed to be a bit tighter than regular pants. The M/L would have been way too big. The cons to the leggings are that they are long and not dryer friendly. I am short so pants are longer on me. I would use that for your context on whether or not to buy based on the length issue. I wore long fuzzy sleeper socks with them when I wore the leggings for the first time. It fixed the issue for me.  Also, you can wear boots with them to cover it up. The other is con is do not put them in the dryer because they are probably going to shrink. They do not provide a lot of stretch. I put them on the hand wash cycle and hung them out to dry in the bathroom. I don't put my leggings in the dryer anyway. Easy solution for that type of situation. I would recommend these leggings to anyone wanting to add uniqueness and color to their leggings collection. I found easy solutions to the very small cons. I love these leggings.

review_summary: I am curvy and so it is t
review_text: I am curvy and so it is tough to find a blazer that is just right.  Everything can hang off you like a a burlap sack or cling to you in the worst places, so I had high hopes for this blazer to replace a gray jersey blazer I already have.The fabric is a bit weird, more stiff than I imagined but not bad.  If nothing else, it gave a cheap blazer more of a dressy appearance, so that was fine.  The Charcoal color is a nice and flattering gray. I have a small waist, a large bust, and medium to large hips.  I also have large upper arms but not wide shoulders.  This blazer fit well everywhere but the arms, which made the shoulders raise up like it had shoulder pads.  Even if I split the liner for a little bit of wiggle room, I didn't think it was an improvement over the blazer I already have.  For me, the button was perfectly placed under my bust and when buttoned really superbly flattered my waist.  However, the aforementioned arm issue meant that the fabric was bring pulled upward and kept bunching and raising out of that perfect spot every single time I moved my arms.I'm actually willing to try this out in another color and wonder if the arms will loosen up after a bit of wear.  It is cute and flattering for short torsos, but I personally ended up returning it.

review_summary: I love the fit and style
review_text: I love the fit and style of this top! I'm a big fan of the cowl style like this one for fall. It not only keeps the breeze out, it adds that little bit of style to an otherwise comfy casual sweater, making it work appropriate (at least for me!).My only complaint is that the fabric is a little bit cheap and pills quite easily, though a quick run over with the sweater shaver (or razor, let's be honest) and it looks almost new again! The Airport Greeting Cardi is part of my favourite #cosycombos with a pair of skinny printed pants in a lowkey plaid, similar to Modcloth's own Books and Crannies Pants (http://www.modcloth.com/shop/pants/booksandcranniespants). Add a contrasting coloured ballet flat (or bootie for better warmth in fall) for some excitement to liven up some dreary Vancouver days. Although this cardigan doesn't really need it due to the cowl, fall is the time I like to layer on the fabric around my neck and shoulders, so I often wear this cardi over my loose cowlneck sweater (that looks a lot like ModCloth's Scenic Route Ready Sweater: http://www.modcloth.com/shop/pulloverssweaters/scenicroutereadysweater) and leave the cardi open a little at the neck to show the fabric contrast. Or, pair this cardigan with a woven tunic like the Back Road Bramble in Black for less business around your neck and shoulders and a more polished look with the whole outfit.

These tops are comfortable and fun to wear, but the manufacturer does seem to have a problem with consistent sizing and quality control. If you order any of these in whatever color, I recommend ordering two, so that at least one will be as you want it. I ordered this color in medium and large. The medium was defective, as it appeared to have been made from pieces from two different sizes. One side was a medium, and the other side was small. It was very lopsided. The large was correctly made, and though a little on the looser side, still a good fit, so I kept the Large and returned the Medium. I also have this top in Petal and Blue Stripe. They do need a little ironing, and I will probably shorten the bottom hem, but aside from the frustrating quality control problem, I really like these light, breezy tunics.

review_summary: I shrieked when I saw thi
review_text: I shrieked when I saw this cardigan was being offered in plus sizes. I've woefully admired this from afar ever since it's debut on the site and finally it's mine! It's definitely a cardigan, not a jacket or a hoodie. Nice and light weight, easy to layer with light weight tees and tanks, and actually looks pretty cute under a medium weight leather jacket. I find it looks great done all the way up, as pictured in the photo or hanging open. Allinall, super flattering. The seaming on the back is something I didn't even really notice in the photos, and it just makes everything that much better in person. I sized up to a 4X, even though I figured a 3X would be alright. 3X would have been fine too, as I do have a little extra room. However, if I had gone with the 3X I wouldn't be able to wear anything bulkier than a thin tee or tank underneath: the cardigan would function more as a primary shirt than a layering piece (if that makes sense?!). The 4X is a little baggy, but because the cardigan is so structured it's a nonissue. I think once I put it in the wash it may shrink a bit too, so I'm not too worried about it. Now I'm thinking about getting in the charcoal... and if it were to be offered in the red or forest green too... well, I don't know if I'd be able to stop myself.

It's such a perfect goto for any event. I've thrown a blazer or cardigan over it and worn it to work, but you can also go out in it at night and look great. It's also great in spring, fall, or summer weather! I've ordered probably 40+ things from modcloth over the years and this has been one of the maybe 3 items that has stuck around as a staple of my wardrobe. I'm at a heavier weight now so it's pretty formfitting but still EXTREMELY flattering. Her dress is Haute Here in The Field. According to the size suggestion, my dress runs a little large.  I usually get a large but I ordered a size down, as suggested, and it was perfect.  The color is outstanding, and the fabric is worth the money. The length was a little long for my taste (almost down to my ankles because I'm only 5' 2), but that wasn't a problem, because I hemmed it up to my knees.  I received many compliments on the dress, that day. It made me feel real girlygirl.  As you can see, I tried to keep with the 60?s look by teasing up my hair and having flipped ends.  My accessories (tear drop earrings, bangle, purse and strappy shoes) were soft pink.  My daughter is in the picture with me in her new dress too.  She also loves her dress (you can see her review in the Haute Here in The Field review section)  Thank you, ModCloth for making our day special.Margo

review_summary: Am I the only woman in th
review_text: Am I the only woman in the world who still likes thick denim?  Haha. These jeans are more thin and stretchy than I would have liked, but I know that's how skinnies are made nowadays.  I would have preferred them to be just a tad darker, too, but the color is fine.  They are a nice dark blue, just not SUPER dark. Also, I think I would have preferred silver buttons over the bright gold, but that's not really a big deal to me.  So, fit Most of the reviews say to order up a size.  I am normally a size 3/4 and ordered a 5 in these.  I feel like I probably should have gone with the 3. The 5's aren't *too* large, but there's definitely some wiggle room (and we all know stretchy pants stretch out over time). In my opinion, if you want these jeans to fit you like they are shown on the model, you should probably order your normal size.  With regard to the waist, I like where it falls.  I actually expected it to be an even higher rise (it hits right at my navel, and I am short waisted).  The length is also good for me, but I have longish legs.  More petite ladies will probably need to hem or cuff them.

The neckline is wide, so it you are uncomfortable with potentially flashing some bra, just wear a cami underneath.  It's light, great fabric, all in all super cute.

I absolutely adore this sweater! It's incredibly soft and cofortable. It was a bit bigger than I expected, but seeing as oversized sweaters are the trend right now, I have no complaints! It's thin, but also very warm. I love layering it with other shirts! Here's hoping May 15th is a breezy day so I can wear this to the movie premiere!

Ordering from Australia, I felt I was taking a bit of a risk with these jeans but they looked so good and I really wanted some high waisted denim.And working out a size was almost traumatic! I am an Aussie 10 and quite tall, so usually go with US size 6 but as they said size up I went from a 7 to a 9 and prayed they fit when I got them.Well, they do and really well. The length is generous on me and there is plenty of stretch. They hug you in all the right places and are quite light in weight which is great for wearing in summer too. Love the indigo colour too. LOVE them. So happy!

Firstly: LOVE it.  Not exactly what I expected but really lovely.Fabric: I expected more of a gray solid, but it is almost a chambray (white one direction and gray the other thread direction) which gives a nice overall effect.Fit: I'm a little bit columnar (read: not much thinner in the waist vs hip) so the fit is quite slim through the middle, so I'm very glad I got the large!  I also have slightly wide shoulders, but the shoulders and cap sleeves are perfect!Length: I expected knee length but it's more of a tea length, I'm 5' 8 and it's below my knee cap.
One of my new favorites! The hard choice is whether to wear it to work or save it for the weekend...
This is a beautiful skirt but you can't go wrong with this style. It's very flattering and a great colour. I usually go for medium but sized up to a large and it fits great.

Beautiful, flattering shirt. The color is gorgeous, very much the blue shown in the photo. The fit is very flattering and the length will be long enough on some women to pull it off with leggings (I'm 5'6) but for me it was a tad too short for that. I love that it's 100% cotton, it's a very comfortable shirt. As far as fit, I normally wear a Large but sized down to a Medium on this one and it worked out great. It is a tad snug across the bust area (I am a C cup) but in a flattering, the girls are under here sort of way, haha. This could be worn alone or with a cute belt. I'd definitely buy again or recommend this top!
Bought this for my daughter and she just loves it. It's a cascade cardigan when fully unzipped.

I'm very conflicted about the sizing of this dress:  on one hand, I do not have to lower the side zipper to slip it on, which means that the dress fits loosely around the waist; on the other hand, I cannot get the buttons to close without my chest looking like it's going to break out of the dress.  Even unbuttoned, there is a slight strain under the bust area, so this dress will always have to be worn with a camisole.  I don't want to size up and be utterly lost elsewhere in the dress, but I don't believe anyone with a bust larger than a B can fit into the top in small small.  Also, I have relatively small shoulders and arms, and the cap sleeves are slightly tight around them. The material of the dress arrived wrinkled and I foresee it will be a constant battle to keep it unwrinkled.  It is hard to get the collars to stay in place.  The dress comes to just under my knee, which is a tad long for me, so I believe I will have it altered.  The color is indeed a mustard, and contrary to other Asian posters, looks well with my skin.  The sash will most likely go unused because as other posters have mentioned, the fabric of the dress is very light and crumples easily  to get that perfect bow, you will most likely have to line it with another fabric or starch the heck out of it.It is a super cute dress though, so despite all these doubts, I will probably keep it.

I am very petite, so the short length works really well for me. I have narrow shoulders, so sometimes I do have to readjust the neckline when it falls towards one shoulder. I should add that my first Write Your Bestseller tee came with a hole in the neckline; Modcloth customer service (as always) was very accomodating and immediately sent me a new shirt, which I received in just a couple of days. The new shirt was perfect in every way. Thanks again, Modcloth!

The challenge is what to do about a bra. Without one, it is too clingy/revealing except for in the most comfortable company. Cross straps on a convertible bra work but the cross over on mine is perfectly framed in the mid cutout on back. Bright contrastcolor straps worn with intent are O.K. but kinda ruin the line. My solution a coobietype bra with straps removed, fashion taped to the top, inside, so it won't slip. Sounds cumbersome, but because I am small busted it works like a dream without buying new underwear. oh and I sized down so the arm holes wouldn't be too droopy.

review_summary: I went back and forth end
review_text: I went back and forth endlessly over whether to get the size small or medium in this jacket after reading other reviews. I opted to (as I normally do) size up. I am very happy with the medium. It fits me exactly like the jacket fits the model on the site, somewhat fitted, but by no means tight. I was worried about the Small not being long enough for me (I'm 5'8) and I was pleasantly surprised to find that this jacket was more than long enough for me! So if you are going on length alone, a size small would have still fit my long arms and torso. I wear this at least a few times a week and I always get lots of compliments on it. This style is hard to come by, so even though I initially thought that $63 was a little steep for an embellished sweatshirt, I would happily pay it again if I lost mine tomorrow. It's so unique without being loud, and the amount of wear I have gotten out of it already has more than justified the price.

review_summary: I wore this dress as the

They are downright charming!Now, it does run a bit small and the fabric is pretty thin, however, it was perfect for the June wedding.  The fabric isn't cheap, but it is thin.  The belt does seem cheap, even though it's made of the same material as the rest of the dress, and the only reason I wore it at all was to give the little loops on each side of the dress a purpose.  Next time I wear it, I'm planning on finding a thin black belt to wear instead of the included one.Overall, my first ModCloth purchase was an incredible success and I am now a ModClother for life!  :)

Fortunately I happen to have some left over from a sewing project that I never really started. I like the coat enough that I may endeavor to add some insulation to it. This is my favorite dress. I absolutely adore it. The delicate blush coloring paired with the lacy fabric underneath and the light, flowy layer of sheer makes you feel like a fairy princess when you put it on. This dress is also soooo comfortable. The sheer fabric is not itchy on your neck or underarms at all, which I was a little apprehensive about when I ordered the dress. It's very soft, the fabric is stretchy, and the tie closure at the neck allows you to make it as tight or as loose at you want around your neck. For a pretty shapeless dress, it was so flattering. The bottom layer of fabric is more shapely so it shows your figure nicely, and the loose sheer fabric on top drapes over your curves. It's very dainty and modest, but also accentuating in the right places. Also, for a tall girl like me, the length was perfect, and I could even wear it with heels. I bought the dress for my homecoming dance, but I love it because it can be dressed up or dressed down! I have also worn it for my senior pictures, a dress casual awards banquet, and just to school somedays with a jean jacket and some tights. It's effortless dressup with heels and nice jewelry or comfy to dress down with cute ankle boots and a cardigan. The best thing is that it's so unique. There aren't a lot of dresses out there similar to this one, and whenever I wear it I always recieve tons of compliments. I normally don't like to spend 100$ on a dress but without a doubt, this one was worth it! #ReviewContest

review_summary: A Must Have for any Book Lover!
review_text: My favorite thing about this dress is the print! It is perfect for any book lover. The colors are right in line for fall and the print itself is subtle enough that those who aren't usually comfortable with bold patterns can still enjoy it. The fit of the waist is a little bit snug, but not uncomfortable. The dress is made of a lightweight cotton so it will work for warmer weather, plus it is fully lined and has pockets (a big plus for teachers!) As a ModStylist, I think this look is super cute with a blouse with fun collar detail layered underneath, such as our Vanilla Milk Top.

Size Ordered:  Small.  The colours of this blouse are fantastic and I like the tunic length.  However, this blouse did not fit me properly:  It was too tight here, but too lose there, uncomfortable here, not sitting right there etc... .   It just isn't designed for me.  I simply wasn't able to get it to work on and on me, in addition to it not fitting anywhere properly it just looked sloppy on me.  This blouse is going to have to go back, but it will definitely look great on the right girl.

Beautiful, beautiful, beautiful! I'd been eyeing this dress in all its colors for quite some time now, and finally broke down and bought it. Worth every penny. It will seriously flatter any shape or size... I'm an hourglass, and it looks really great on me. A little too great, maybe haha. It's actually a little sexy for work, but I'll manage (maybe I should have gone one size up? It emphasizes my chest a bit. Something to be aware of if you've got something up top!)The color is absolutely gorgeous, AND the material is surprisingly high quality. It has a nice, soft texture, and feels very sturdy  this dress will definitely last for some time. It's true that the buttons are a little cheap and I think they might fall off before too long... buuut I personally think they look nice with the dress and complement it well. It's also a little short, so I wear it with leggings for work (and I'm only 5'4), but it looks great all the same. I'm so thrilled about this purchase  get this dress, you won't regret it! :)

review_summary: I adore this dress!  I wo
review_text: I adore this dress!  I wore it to an October wedding in Virginia, and it was perfect. I have NEVER gotten so many compliments from strangers.  The other reviewers are right that the fabric is a little thicker/winterweight, but for a cool fall evening, this was perfect.  Also, the fabric is stretchy, so if you're like me and don't fall uniformly into one size, it might still fit.  The way that the lace is positioned made me look supersexy and curvy, but with the high neck and kneeish length, it still felt appropriate for a church and country club.  It was comfortable to move in (b/c of the stretchiness).  My only (minor) complaint is that I would've liked the lace to go all the way around, but as another reviewer mentioned, it's pretty reasonably priced, so that's a small sacrifice.  Overall, I am so so happy with this dress.  Can't wait to wear it again!  P.S.  I accessorized with a nude/tan clutch, nude/tan wedges, and a nude/tan linen wrap, and that seemed to work well.  I also opted for an updo so that I wouldn't cover up any of the beautiful lace. :)

These are practically perfect in every way! They've held up to almost daily wear during one (Canadian) winter and occasional wear during the summer. They repel water, stains, and even snags (and I work in a kitchen), don't stretch out between even infrequent washings and are flatteringly smoothing, and are also opaque, pretty, and shimmery in the light. The waistband is a little tight by the end of the day, but doesn't budge at all even if it folds over, which I definitely prefer to hiking them up all the time! They cover my ankles and land at my natural waist, so they might not fit you right if you get the same size but are shorter or taller. They're almost magical in that they are warm enough for winter but somehow breathable in the summer. Buy them now. They're definitely worth the price!

review_summary: I wanted this dress for y
review_text: I wanted this dress for years. First, they didn't make Luck Be A Lady in the larger sizes. Then they finally started making them in bigger sizes, but still I hesitated. I was concerned by the reviews that said this dress may not work for bustier girls. Finally gave in and ordered the dress, and man, those reviewers couldn't be more wrong. I'm a 38K (US) in bras, and this dress both has plenty of room up top and it's super flattering. It gives my figure a nice balance with its narrow waist and full skirt, counteracting my naturally topheavy appearance. This dress is (just barely) long enough for work. I recently bought the green luck be a lady, and discovered that different colors come in different lengths. This one is about 2 longer than the green one, meaning I can get away without tights under this dress. All in all, these are now my favorite dresses, and I'll probably buy more colors yet. Highly recommend.

I was wrong.  I was beyond excited to get this shirt because of all the great reviews and everyone looked so cute in it in their pics, but I was swimming in the material.  Not only that but the tie around the waist made my chest look massive and saggy, which no girl wants.  I'm sure if I had bought the medium, I probably wouldn't have had these issues and I'd be sporting this shirt seven days a week.

review_text: i like this blouse! the sleeves are longer and have more puff at the shoulder than i'd envisioned but its probably how it fits my particularly short frame. the fabric is soft and the bow is adorable! my only real grievance with it are the buttonholes which came to me already fraying but this shirt slips overhead easily for me so im not going to fuss with the buttons

review_summary: So, my husband wins all t
review_text: So, my husband wins all the awards; he caught me looking longingly at this dress and bought it for me for my 30th birthday!The fit runs a bit on the large side; it actually doesn't cling to my chest like I'm used to. That being said, I don't mind that because I can a) wear a pushup bra without worrying about the waist coming up to my neck and b) can layer underneath the dress for the winter! The skirt reaches my knees exactly, and I'm a rather stumpy 5'4. I rock this look with some vintage brass jewelry (to match the buttons), heathered dark grey tights, brown equestrian boots, and a vintage brown flight jacket thrown over the top.

The first thing I noticed was that the quality of the polyester fabric is amazing! I usually only wear natural fabrics, but this dress drapes beautifully. I get compliments on this dress everywhere I go! Cinch it with an elastic belt, toss on some heels, and I am gone!

review_summary: When I saw this on the si
review_text: When I saw this on the site I had to have it, and it was a good buy. I wear it all the time. The fabric is some kind of voodoo  it's warm when I need it to be and cool when it's hot out, and you can tell when you tug at it that it's really durable and highquality. It is a little seethrough, but not a ton. I'm fine with a fleshcolored bra under it and you can't make out my tattoos, which is my standard for sheer. The mandarin collar is super cute. My only things are that the bit of fabric that the buttons are attached to is hanging free, so it tends to curl up, but you can fix that with a single stitch. Also, it can tend to kind of billow out if you're just relaxing in it, which isn't that flattering, but it tends to be fine when I'm on my feet. Also for me personally it's a little tight across the bust, but, well, everything is tight across the bust on me.

The skirt fit great, but the waist, chest, shoulders, and sleeves of this dress were way too big. I have wide shoulders, but a narrow ribcage. I'd order a size down, but I worry it won't fit through the hips. I'm returning this.

review_summary: I LOVE this coat! Love it
review_text: I LOVE this coat! Love it! It's super adorable and I get compliments on it all the time. Be forewarned that the hood is very huge  I only wear the hood up if I am walking straight forward because of its size, but boy does it come in handy on a cold/windy day. The hood adds to the cute factor, too. The coat keeps me nice and warm, too: on a normal Minnesota winter day, I can wear it with or without a scarf (because the hood kind of acts as its own scarf!) over my normal clothes and stay perfectly warm. On a really really cold day, I wear a hat under the hood and wear layers, but that's no different than any other cold weather outfit I would wear with any winter coat. Seriously, you need this coat!
Overall I love this shirt! It's wonderfully light and cool. The top scoop moves more than I want and can hang down lower than I want for teaching, but that's why I wear a cami underneath.

review_summary: not at all as cute as pictured
review_text: I LOVE all things mint/turquoise and had high hopes for this, with its lovely little flutter sleeves and gauzy outer fabric. But it's going right back. I'm petite (5' 3 and 125 lbs.), but this made me look quite dumpy. Even on my short torso, the waistline is too high. The waist seam is also quite thick and bunchylooking and hangs too loosely around the waist(there's a reason the front view in the ad has the model with her arms covering her waist!).The flutter sleeves hit at an unflattering spot on my upper arms (and I usually find flutter sleeves very flattering). The dress arrived with a small stain on the skirt that I couldn't remove. It is also dryclean only (which is listed in the item description)and I only want to go to that hassle if I LOVE the dress. Not quite how something that looked so perfect in the picture ended up so dumpy inperson.

As with the majority of fit and flarestyle dresses, I found that the waist on this dress was at least two inches too high for my long torso, and so it was really huge on my waist and hips. The top was a bit too tight on my shoulders and chest, which was surprising because I tend to be on the small side of xs. The material is nice and so is the color, though, and it's a good length, just above the knee.

Please see my measurements.  I shift from a 1X 2X depending on how it is made.  This shirt (I have in 4 different colors)  is long and spacious.  If you are looking for a true tunic, well here ya go.  I ordered a 1X and it was still quite big. So I dried one of my older ones in the dryer and drew up slightly but is still a true tunic just not as roomy as before.  I have now dried all 4 of them in the dryer and still love the fit.  This is a good shirt, good quality and looks great with skinny jeans, a long necklace and heels.  Flip side, It also looks great at home with comfy leggings and flipflops.  If you don't want it to look like a nigh shirt, DEFIANTLY size down.

review_summary: This top is super cute an
review_text: This top is super cute and comfy. Most shirt buttons pull in the bust area, but this one really doesn't which is great. My only gripe is it being 100% polyester. I would be a bit happier with the price line if it were silk but the puppies are so cute that I'm willing to overlook it!

First ever ModCloth purchase.  There were a lot of obstacles that happened between the order placement and the hoodie actually appearing on my doorstep, but ModCloth's customer service was amazing the whole way through.  The first Leipzig hoodie I received actually had many of the problems identified throughout these 700+ (!!) reviews: a weird chemical smell, numerous frayed threads, a very rough feel, tight fit, and worst of all, multiple holes in the sleeves akin to those that form after a few washes, or numerous moth attacks.  It felt like a mockup of the actual product.However,  ModCloth's AMAZING customer service responded almost immediately and overnighted me a new one, which is in much better condition.  It's almost unbelievable how different the two jackets were.Still, the quality is lessthanideal for such an expensive item, and it isn't terribly warm; but it is comfortable, and I am enjoying wearing it.No regrets here.  Thanks, ModCloth.

review_summary: This dress was the most a
review_text: This dress was the most amazing thing, but incredibly hard to size correctly. This was the ONLY dress I wanted for my Harry Potter themed wedding. I usually am an 8  10, but had to purchase this dress in a 16. All smaller sizes would fit well all over, but the shoulders and sleeves would not fit. There is no give whatsoever in the sleeves. I had to size up, then have it altered at the waist. The sleeves then fit perfect in the shoulders, but the bottom of the sleeve gaped some. The sleeves were only slightly itchy to me  they were not a problem at all. The dress is beautiful and I would absolutely buy it again, even with the sizing issues. I think it is better to just size up and have it altered, if you are having issues with the fit of the sleeves.

It's actually too big but I love that because most hoods don't cover your whole head. I bought this coat for a trip in April to Ireland (when it's cold and rainy) and I can't wait to wear it there!

review_summary: This is my first Mod Clot
review_text: This is my first Mod Cloth purchase and I'm in love! I found this dress to be the perfect length despite my 510 height and the fabric is delightfully soft and comfortable. This dress hugs in all the right places and makes you feel like a goddess :)  tried this on straight after a 10 hr work shift and my husband's comment was 'Wow that looks amazing! You look like such a knockout and like you just spent 5 hours getting ready to go out' which from him is a huuuuuge reaction. The only slight drawback and I do mean slight... was that this fabric is a little thin so if you're wearing a bra with any lace or texture detail on it the texture will make the bust appear a bit bumpy. Basically wear a bra that's nice and smooth and you're fine :) All in all I LOVE this dress and can't wait to wear it to our friend's wedding and then absolutely everywhere! I will definitely be buying from Mod Cloth again :D

review_summary: This is one of the skirts
review_text: This is one of the skirts I've been wanting for the longest time and finally decided to give in and buy it. It was everything I imagined it to be and thanks to reading the other reviews and going with my instinct; I was able to determine which size worked for me. Once I was finally able to receive my package ( even though it was late due to some weird handling by postal service), I immediately tried it on and it fit comfortably with a tad bit of extra space for tucking in shirts. I love the color, the fit and the length! Really, really happy with this purchase. :)

This coat is perfect! Fits perfect, material is sort of fleece/woollike without actually being wool, which is good because it's not scratchy and no little sheep had to suffer. I love that the back is slightly longer than the front, and kind of flouncy...makes it very feminine. Dressy enough to be worn on special occasions, but also casual enough to wear every day. The buttons are sewn on well also, and after wearing this coat for about a month they don't seem to be getting loose, which is good. A+++!

My biggest concern for this coat was that the arms would be too tight. I tend to carry a lot of weight in my arms and chest, so I always have to size up in coats. This particular coat fits as perfectly as I could hope in those spots, and the belt cinches the waist down quite nicely. There are pockets hidden in the front two pleats, which is also a bonus I didn't expect based on some previous reviews I read.  The coat is heavier than I expected as well. I think it will hold up nicely in the winters where I live.  I haven't had a chance to test it in the rain yet. Overall I am very pleased with this coat.

review_summary: Positives and negatives, but very clearly not the dress for
review_text: This dress has a warm, antique look to it, but unfortunately that just washed me out. The netting has a yellowish tinge, and the lace is a light brown (sort of tea stained). The white lining underneath the netting can be seen, which makes for an interesting effect with the three colors (white, yellow, brown). That said, I think I might prefer a more monochromatic look. I also found the bodice to be too large. I could zip it up with no problem while wearing a bra, but that defeats the purpose of the builtin cups and made me look enormous. However, the shoulders fit perfectly, meaning I couldn't size down. The hem stopped around my ankles, which I didn't end up liking. One thing that was nice was that the lace collar lay flat (unlike the collar for the Exquisite Elegance). Also, the lace was extremely soft, much more so than the Exquisite Elegance.Given how big the bodice was on me, I recommend sizing down if you're on the border between sizes or at least anticipate some alterations. I also recommend adding a small hook and eye closure to the side zipper.

I'm a clothing designer and seamstress...when something fits weird, it REALLY bugs me. So, here are my two biggest issues with this jacket  I'll let you decide for yourself if it's worth the $55 + tax and shipping.1) I ordered a Small, and despite being fairly true to size in all other proportions, the fit in the shoulders and armholes was off by enough that I not only noticed it, but couldn't keep wearing it because it bothered me so much. The armholes are cut improperly, which feels really awkward when worn, like my shoulders are being forced back because there's not enough room in the front of the armscye and too much in the back. I was tempted to rip the sleeves out and recut the armscye myself, but that's opening a whole other can of worms and I'd rather just order a size Medium and see if the fit across the shoulders doesn't improve when I go up a size.2) The quality of the material is not as nice as I would have expected for something of this price. The trouble is that this jacket has a synthetic type of jersey knit that makes me worry that it's going to start pilling anywhere there's friction (under the arms mainly). Sure it looks nice when you pull it out of the bag, but check the armpits after a month of regular wear and tell me it's not pill city under there. Since fit and quality are my two most important expectations from a garment, I'm sad to say this jacket did not deliver on either account so it's going back.

I purchased a larger size so I could layer sweaters during the season so I feel like I might be swimming a tad when I'm not layered to the max. The angle of the zipper is definitely not as extreme as shown but it's still a nice change from the regular straight up and down zipper. In my case, my shorts do gap a bit between the middle two buttons, even though the rest of the fit is great. I considered exchanging for a medium, but I think the fit would've been looser than I want. The gap bothers me a bit, but such is the cost of laziness. :) Overall, I agree with other reviews  you will not regret purchasing these shorts!

review_summary: Pretty color, love the pleating, weird fit, needed alteratio
review_text: I bought/wore this as a bridesmaids dress.  I am 5'4, 126 lbs, pear shaped, and wore heels.  I bought a medium and it fit well except the top/bust was too long/large (and I am a ccup) and the overall length of the dress was too long (that is normal for me though).  I had straps altered/taken in which fixed the top and the overall length, so a pretty simple alteration.  I really liked the vneck as it did not show too much.  The dress was very comfortable and flowy.  One weird thing that I did not like was the seam at the bottom of the waist  it showed too much (see close up photo).  Also, where the fabric changed at the bottom of the drop waist to the pleated fabric brought more attention to the hips (which I was not a fan of). Overall the dress was pretty and fit well after some alterations.  PS the colors in my personal photos are weird, and the dress color was pretty true to the photos modcloth uses.

Very cute design, but fabric is very cheap, the kind that pulls and snags easily and then leaves a long run on a garment (kind of like snagged pantyhose). This didn't bother me so much though it was the snagged and short threads on the seams of the garment that looked the worst. Wherever there is a seam where you can see the stitching on the outside of the garment (like on the button band straight down the front), there were tiny snags and gathers and puckers in the thread. It is the effect you see on either a poorly made garment or an extremely old garment that has been washed so many times, the thread starts to fall apart and disintegrate. If you only planned on wearing this top underneath a sweater, you wouldn't notice the poor construction. If you plan on wearing it by itself, this might be pretty noticeable. Also, since I don't recall anyone complaining about this before, it's possible I just got a damaged top. Either way, I will be returning this item.

review_summary: This is probably one of m
review_text: This is probably one of my easiest purchases at ModCloth because of customer reviews and photos. I was really excited to buy this, but unfortunately, it wasn't as perfect as how I imagined it. It's sort of loose on the underarm area, as if it's not made to be an offshoulder top. It only looks good when I pull the shoulder section up a little more than I should. Just imagine a Vneck shirt being worn as an offshoulder top. That's similar looking to how it fits on me?loose on the underarm section but everything is fitting. In the picture, I'm not sure if it's noticeable, but the fit is a bit different from the ModCloth model. If I pull it up a little more, that's the only time it perfectly fits on me. I lost weight, so I am not sure if the size I got has to do with it but I believe I didn't lose as much so I should still be a medium. All in all I think this is a nice top. Not something you can find anywhere (that also fits you very well). However, keep in mind it still differs body to body so it may look better if you have a bigger chest? I'm thinking that may be the reason as to why it is a little loose on my underarm area.

I found this dress while looking for something to get for my cousin's black tie wedding. It really is a beautiful dress. It fits kind of tight, esp. around the bum, but not in such a way that it's not supposed to (if that makes sense). Also the length is long enough that it doesn't ride up to make you selfconscious that you're showing off anything you don't want to in public. Those folds along the waist are GREAT because the fabric is so soft and comfortable that without them you'd have a tummy bump but with them it's so stylish and pretty. The only problem I have is that it gives you a nice sexy bum bump like the pencil skirts Sandra Bullock wore in The Blind Side which isn't my thing. Anyway, the fit is nice, the length is cool and it lands right around/above my knees. I thought it was beautiful from the front but I had to return it.

My story: Usually I avoid anything that covers up my collar bones, anything without sleeves, or a full skirt so naturally I decided to avoid this dress like the plague.After looking through the raving reviews however I got so curious and it look amazing in the photos so I put it in my shopping bag where it stayed for a week until I got the courage up to just buy and and give it a shot.Results: NEW. FAVORITE. DRESS.  The materiel is perfect. It's heavy enough to give it a good weight when it's on my body so it won't bunch up. It's light enough to be worn on the hottest day in New Orleans, and with thick tights and ankle high boots it'll make a transition into the fall and winter. I do not know how I lived without this dress.  LBD is gonna move over because this is the new must have!!Color: looking at it on retina display  I thought the color was brilliant, and in person it's even better. I got so excited once I put it on and seen how well it complimented my Ariel red hair, and pale pale skin.  I've got to say with the red hair, the purple and the pale skin I /had/ to add green shoes to give it a little mermaid feel. It's so perfect.Bonus thoughts: I can also see our First Lady wearing this dress. I'm honestly waiting on her to wear the coach tour dress soon so if you're on the fence about this like I was... Go for it!

The biggest issue for me was the waistband gap above my butt  usually, stretchier jeans with a higher waistband entirely eliminate that problem, but these were the exception.  If I sized down, I'm fairly certain that wouldn't be a problem anymore, but then the pants would be stretched too tightly across my hips/seat.Additionally, the material is just too thin for my liking.  I live in the Northeast US and usually layer leggings under my jeans for extra warmth in the winter, but these feel too much like a base layer.  This also makes it hard for me to tuck in shirts without making the pants look rumpled.I will say though, that the rear pocket placement is absolutely perfect.

I am a topheavy, appleshaped woman with a very long torso. I hesitated for a long time before buying this dress (or any garment online) because I couldn't tell if it would be too short, or if the slight gathering at the waist would make me feel like a thirdtrimester pregnant woman (which gathered waists tend to do on me), or if it would look more like an empire waist on me, which makes me look even more like a pregnant woman (as I mentioned, my torso is long and my boobs are huge, so waistlines often sit way too high on me), or if the neckline would be too high (low necklines seem to balance out my topheavy proportions). I took a chance and got it. I love this dress. I almost ordered a 3X, but I'm glad I didn't. It fits with a good deal of ease everywhere, even the waist, where almost all dresses are too tight on me. Most surprisingly, the waist seam sits at my actual natural waist, not right under my boobs like I thought it would. The length is just long enough that I never feel uncomfortable, even when I sit down or even bend over. The armscyes are a little big, though not so much that it looks gross. The print is irresistible. Make sure you get a belt for it,  though (I wear it with a wide elastic belt. It's black, but I'd wear a red one if I could find it). It looks great with bright primarycolored accessories and a short cardigan. And IT HAS POCKETS. The price seems high for basic woven cotton, but I love cotton anyway. It doesn't wrinkle like I thought it would. A+

Overall, I like the Leipzing Hoodie.    I tucked the hood into the collar and it gives it a nice stiffness, not bulky at all. The cuffs have a neat extra wrap around cinch to them, which seems more insulating, but does make a little squeeze on the hands getting in.  As far as quality goes, outside of a few stray threads that needed trimming, the biggest problem I've had is the metal zipper, although not defective, is difficult to use easily, particularly at the connecting point at the bottom.  As far as fit goes, I ordered the Medium, my normal size, and I'm 5'4 and 130135 lbs. 34 bust, 30 waist. The overall length and arm length was perfect, falling 6 below my hip bone and and inch or two past my wrist bone.   I haven't tried washing it yet, so I don't know how the color retention or shrinkage will be, and I'm actually planning on dry cleaning it to avoid those risks.  It's become my daily outerwear for these 65 degree weather days, although I wouldn't trust it to keep me warm below 60 without additional thin thermal layering, not much extra bulk would be able to fit under it.   My boyfriend actually found it and recommended it to me, and he seems pretty impressed and really likes it too.

review_text: I love this dress. I also own it in polka dot, black, red, and blue w/ red buttons. I can wear this in the autumn and spring as is, and pair it with a cardigan and tights in the winter. I can wear this to work, and to dinner and a Broadway show (I live in NYC). I'm 5'7 and usually don't consider myself short by any means, and this was more ankletofloor length than true ankle. I did wear flat shoes, which may have made a difference, but it did work nicely with them, it just wasn't QUITE the length I expected. THAT BEING SAID, this dress was every bit as lovely as I was hoping! I got compliments all night. Our reception was a cocktail hour at a winery and I was comfortable and never felt overthetop bridal compared to my casuallydressed guests. I could see this dress working for a courthouse wedding, or, with the right accessories, being done up for a more traditional ceremony! I do recommend doublesided body tape for the neckline, the center scallop in the fabric likes to move but one strip of tape kept it secure all night!

If you are going to use this coat for special occasions then you can get away with it but if you are using it as your every day winter coat and you live some place like MN, it's going to look real shabby in about a month.  I still loved the style so much that I got the Midnight blue hoping that the dark color would help hide those imperfections.  It did to a degree but still after 6 weeks the coat looked shabby. The fit was exactly as expected and very flattering (which is especially nice since I'm a larger girl), and I've gotten many compliments on it, ranging from a coworker who commented on how pretty it was (a WEEK after I had actually worn it), to people who I didn't even realize were Modclothusers until they point and say THAT'S IT! IT'S *THAT* DRESS!All that said, my only complaint would be that the thread keeping the buttons on is a bit thin/flimsy and trails a bit off the buttons, enough so that one of the buttons came off while in the wash (though to be fair, I probably shouldn't have thrown my shining star in with the rest of the plebeian laundry). Easy fix if you've got a needle and thread, but a little disappointing for a dress that's otherwise wellmade.All THAT said, it's a freaking awesome piece of clothing. Continue to covet, my Modcloth friends, and lay in wait as I did...

This works well. I sized up from medium to large and it fits well (usually medium in tops).I have a bunch of the charter school cardigans and I can say this charcoal one is different. The buttons are a different style and the trim is thicker than the others. It makes it a little different feel but the overall fit seems fairly similar and I still like it quite a bit. I compared it to the ivory charter school cardigan in the same size (and some other colors), also this year's model. The ivory one (MAK  like most of the orig style) has slightly wider shoulder seems, thinner fabric, smaller buttons. This charcoal one (diff designer  fervour same style name), has thick ribbed edging, larger buttons, wider body, longer overall and is made of a softer, maybe slightly thicker fabric. For me, they both work. They do fit a little differently. The charcoal may fit me a little better and is a little comfier.I'd be interested in seeing more colors of this charcoal version. The larger buttons and thicker ribbing could be toned down a bit, but I would guess most people may not notice. It does feel a little more substantial, but the difference is minimal.bottom line: You probably want this as you can wear it with everything.If the trim/buttons and sizing diff might matter to you check the designer first. If you just squeezed into the fervour style (this), order a size up in the MAK version (ivory, other colors).

Overall, it just wasn't the dress for me, which I was sad about since it was so pretty. I honestly think it would probably look better on someone taller than I am (I'm 5'5). review_summary: There isn't much to say a
review_text: There isn't much to say about this cardigan except for the word perfection. I had been looking for a new dress cardigan for ages and I, finally, found it here. I wear tons of vintage and vintage inspired dresses and I end up using this cardigan constantly. I have just ordered the red one actually! I am 5'3 and 130 lbs and I went with the large because I have a large cup size. I didn't want to look like I was wearing a child's sweater and sizing up from my usual medium was a good idea. This is a goto and must have for any avid dresswearer. And it comes in several colors! =) The material is light, but not flimsy. It is flexible and doesn't hinder arm movement whether it is unbuttoned or buttoned up. It keeps the chill off your shoulders in the autumn of Los Angeles. If you're looking for a dress cardigan, you're simply not going to find better. Not even with a bigger price tag. Trust me. I've looked *everywhere*. Keep the colors coming, Mod Cloth! =)

I ordered this jacket based on the amazing reviews and how it looked on the mannequin. When I got the jacket, I was initially happy with it aside from the fact that the pocket flaps curled up on either ends (something that could easily be fixed with an iron, which I do not have :( ). It is very important that you pay attention to the washing instructions and DO NOT put this jacket in the dryer! The first time I washed my jacket, I forgot that it was heat sensitive, so as a result the plastic on the hood strings melted/cracked off and my zippers melted and bent. I understand that this is NOT the jacket's fault! However it was a little irritating. Besides my mistake, the problems that I have with the jacket are in the fit. I have a slim, hourglass figure and generally prefer my clothes to fit snug and create smooth lines against my body. This jacket, although abstract in design fails to fit my body in a flattering way. Rather than fit close to my body, the jacket forms a boxy shape around my midsection, making me look about 510lbs heavier.To conclude, I wish this jacket was offered in XS. I believe that if I could have sized down just one more size (and remembered the washing instructions!) this jacket would have been a staple in my wardrobe. However, this is not the case and I am considering donating it and finding something else that better suits my body type.

The material of this dress is a heavy, brocade like fabric which I am not sure will really work for Texas spring and summers. But who cares, I can definitely wear it during Fall, Winter and early Spring and I'm 100% okay with that since I bough the dress for the print. Busty friends do beware since I found that the bodice is also quite fitted, I'm pretty small chested for my build and even my chest felt compressed in this dress's bodice. I felt to bow was cute for someone younger (teens or early 20s) but in my late 20s I need something a little more mature, hence the belt. I've washed it once, in cold water on the gentle cycle, and hung it out on the line to dry. It washed very well and needed a little ironing after it dried but that's to be expected with this type of fabric. My only caution is that the dress is a little narrow across the shoulders and might not work well for broad shouldered gals.

review_summary: This has got to be the mo

review_summary: I usually wear a 2x in sh
review_text: I usually wear a 2x in shirts and they either look a little baggy or just right. and hoodies usually run small on me because the size of my hips make them tight, so I sized up to a 3x. and holy cow. this thing is so stretchy and large that I could fit a skinny friend in there with me lol so now I've ordered a 1x because there isn't a 2x in stock. hopefully it fits nicely

This dress is so flattering and such a lovely plum colour. I definitely need a slip underneath, as it shows underwear lines if not, but that's not a problem for me. My favourite modcloth purchase to date!

I was really excited for this dress and my husband took the hint and bought it for me for Christmas! Unfortunately I could barely get it on and he had to help me get it off. The silver flecks in the fabric look much cheaper in person. I won't be exchanging it for a L, partly of the quality and partly because I don't have faith that it will fit well either. (I am always a M, this dress runs small). I'm disappointed, but at least I will get some in store credit to buy something else!

I am in LOVE with this dress. It is so elegant and beautiful, especially the neckline. The neckline is a little higher than usual, but the fact that it is unlined makes it sooooo pretty on. This dress is a bit pricey but well worth the money; I can see myself wearing this to multiple parties and events. The lace fabric feels like it is quality and has some weight to it, which is nice. I'm 5'5 and this hits me right at the knee/slightly below the knee. If you're worried about the fit,  I would say to consider sizing down. I ordered my usual size, but because of the elastic waist (which, by the way, hits slightly higher than my natural waist), I could have easily sized down on it. The waist is the most fitted part of the dress; the flared shape of the skirt elegantly glides over hips, so sizing down shouldn't cause you any fit problems on the bottom half.

This dress is phenomenal. The fit is flattering, the neckline is just different enough. The length (for me) didn't feel inappropriately short (I would wear this in summer weather sans tights). The buttons are a simple, not overdone, bit of whimsy, and it can be worn in cold weather with a shirt under it and not ruin the lines of the dress.If you have a slender figure like me, the gathers give a bit of an illusion of being curvierMy favorite part? POCKETS!I plan on nabbing a few more colors.

review_summary: I sat on this dress for a
review_text: I sat on this dress for a few weeks before finally purchasing it, I loved the shape but I was looking for something casual I could wear to school and such. I finally purchased it (on sale!) and it was delivered quickly. Unfortunately I was disappointed when I tried it on, it was HUGE! My measurements are between a 2X and 3X, I went with the 3X as it seemed like it was a pretty formfitting dress. Boy, was I wrong! I could easily get away with the 1X. I highly recommend sizing down (at least for the plus size, sometimes the normal sizes are different). But I will say the dress seemed to be of high quality and seemed as though it would be everything I pictured, in the right size. Unfortunately the other sizes were sold out, so I returned it. But maybe I'll try for the plum purple.

review_summary: These took me a while to
review_text: These took me a while to get used to. At first I wasn't sure I could pull them off tucked in, but I was fine with that, because they did great things for my waist. Carrying most of my weight in my middle, it's hard to find jeans that don't give me that terrible muffin top, but these 100% solve that problem. After having them for a few weeks, I now sometimes even tuck in my shirt, which is great. In short, these make a curvy gal feel confident! I want them in every color now. They aren't super thick, but they don't overstretch either: a good moderateweather pair of jeans. Highly recommended.

review_summary: As soon as I saw these in
review_text: As soon as I saw these incredible pants I had to have them! I'm very short so had to have them shortened. Even after having them tailored, I think they look best with a slight heel or wedge. I am 52  and 115 lbs and generally fit into a size 3 pants. I purchased a 26 and it fit just right in the butt and crotch area (hooray for that!). So far I've worn them out twice and have been amazed by how many people will stop me and ask about them! Thanks for being awesome Mod Cloth!

review_summary: I bought this dress to we
