My husband presented me with this gem of a frock for my VDay/BDay (same day) gift. He was on a tight budget and could only afford one item and when he realized he couldn't spring for faster shipping and still stick to the budget and make sure it would arrive in time for me to open it on my birthday/Valentine's Day... so ModRachel upgraded him to expedited shipping for free, ensuring that the dress would arrive on time. Additionally, it fits perfectly true to size, looks awesome in the dark, and garnered me compliments from pretty much everyone who saw me whilst I was wearing it, including 3 different ladies I subsequently referred to Mod Cloth because they wanted to purchase it for themselves! My only complaint (like many of the other reviewers) is the collar: it looks out of place, wrinkles easily, and doesn't lay flat. I will be removing it (even though I typically love a good Peter Pan collar), and I suggest it be removed from the design altogether.

review_summary: So, I waited a long time
review_text: So, I waited a long time for these jeans to come back into stock in the size 15 and was pretty excited to finally get them. That said, despite being high waisted, they barely covered my butt. I wear a size 10 to 12 normally, and juniors jeans pretty much never have enough room in the thigh or butt areas, but some high rise pants have worked out for me in the past. Not these. According to the size chart, I should wear a 13 given my measurements. Definitely not the case with this brand. review_text: Ok, I first want to say that I LOVE and am obsessed with this coat. I've worn it every day since it arrived about 2 weeks ago. That being said, there's some things about it that almost made me return it. 1. It's very boxy. I have an hourglass shape and it's kind of lost in how straight the midsection of this coat is. I contemplated exchanging it for a size smaller for that very reason but didn't because....2. The arms are incredibly short. They just barely cover my wrists, and I have pretty short arms to begin with, so I figured if I sized down, the sleeves would be even shorter than the 2x. 3. Also, this coat turns my nails blackish blue! Took me a week to realize it was the coat that was doing that to my nails because I work in a tattoo shop and just assumed I kept touching transfer paper or something that was turning them blue... then one day I carried a white bag while wearing the coat and noticed it also got stained from rubbing on the coat. All in all, the coat is amazing quality and super cozy! Love the length too! I intend on having it taken in just a tiny bit to make it more shapely :) I look forward to wearing this coat for many winters!

I really liked this hoodie at first. It was cute and structured and kept me warm in very mildly cold weather. The material isn't soft but I could deal with that. However, this item is deceptive in some ways. It looks like it provides more warmth than it actually does. I live in Northern California, where it doesn't snow but temps regularly drop to the 40s or even 30s(Fahrenheit). In all honesty, this jacket provides barely more warmth than a cardigan. Once the weather started to get in the 40s, I'd have to wear this with a lot of layers. Thankfully the jacket is warm enough to do this (I'm typically a 2x3x and I ordered a 3x). Then after wearing it maybe just a few times a week, it quickly started to fall apart. A few days after it arrived, one of the snaps on the wrist came completely detached, so I had this awkward piece of fabric hanging from my wrist. Then the snaps on the pockets came completely off so now the pockets hang open, making it look really bulky. And then the cords started to unravel because the metal pieces on the end fell right off. A jacket that I've only had for about 2 months looks like something I've owned for 10 years because it's so damaged. I am not rough with my clothes, I wasn't doing manual labor with it on, and I didn't even undo or pull on the snaps or cords at all. I do not recommend this item for that reason. The quality is nowhere near what it needs to be to justify the price.

These shorts were everything I had been looking for. The fit was great, the length was even better, they were stretchy but still had form and this high rise was the greatest thing to ever come back into style. But the quality is beyond sad. They didn't even last past my test run of wearing them around the house. About 10 minutes into wearing them for the first time one of the buttons just drops to the ground. I was confused because there was no stress on the button holes... If anything I could have gone down a size (I'm normally a 12 but the 1x is closer to the fit of a 14) but I was looking for more of a loose fit. So, in order to get my money's worth from these I had to superglue the metal button back together just to hold them on. I'm very disappointed because this is my second order that didn't even last me multiple wears. At this point the only thing keeping me coming back is the outstanding customer service that Modcloth provides.

I have very conflicted feelings about this hoodie. On one hand, I love the way it looks and feels. It's warm and comfortable but looks so much nicer than just a regular hoodie. As far as the look goes, it's perfect. But functionally, it has issues. For all the snaps and zips, you only get two pockets in this thing everything else is just an accessory. Why would you do that? If you're already putting zippers on a hoodie, why wouldn't you put real pockets underneath? But the worst part was the zipper. It's easy enough to zip but impossible to unzip. The mechanism isn't right, there's extra metal on the bottom keeping you from pulling the zipper apart to take it off. I eventually had to do major surgery on it and now I think it works okay but why did I have to seriously take a knife and wire cutters to a $60 jacket? That seems ridiculous. This hoodie is super cute and I'm sure I'll wear it a lot but it just seems like you should get more for your money.
